title: heliotropium earlei
intro: Heliotropium earlei, also known as the Earle's heliotrope, is a flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Heliotropium earlei include relieving congestion, reducing inflammation, and soothing the respiratory tract. Herbal preparations of Heliotropium earlei are often made into teas, tinctures, or infused oils, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
